note_data.title: Describing motion
keyPoints: **Speed** is a scalar quantity that has magnitude only and no direction
**Velocity** is a vector quantity that has both magnitude and direction
**Acceleration** is the rate of change of velocity and is measured in m/s²
On a **distance-time graph**, the gradient gives the speed of the object
On a **velocity-time graph**, the gradient gives the acceleration and the area under the graph gives the distance travelled
**Uniform acceleration** occurs when velocity changes by equal amounts in equal time intervals
At the highest point of vertical motion, velocity is zero but acceleration due to gravity is still 9.8 m/s² downward
**Average speed** equals total distance divided by total time taken

definitions.definition: The distance travelled per unit time
The displacement per unit time in a given direction
The rate of change of velocity
The distance moved in a particular direction from a reference point

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one fact in one sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the point + reason using 'because' or 'this is because'
Show the formula, substitute values, and give the final answer with correct unit
State what happens at different stages of the motion

modelAnswer.question: A bus travelling from Lahore to Islamabad covers 120 km in the first 2 hours, then stops for 30 minutes, and covers the remaining 150 km in 2.5 hours. Calculate the average speed for the entire journey.
modelAnswer.answer: Total distance = 120 + 150 = 270 km. Total time = 2 + 0.5 + 2.5 = 5 hours. Average speed = total distance ÷ total time = 270 ÷ 5 = 54 km/h

commonMistakes: Never confuse speed with velocity - speed has no direction, velocity does
Students often forget to include stopping time when calculating average speed
Never read velocity-time graphs as distance-time graphs - check the y-axis label carefully
Students often say acceleration is zero at the top of projectile motion - velocity is zero but acceleration due to gravity continues
Never use speed when the question asks for velocity - always include direction for velocity
examinerTips: Motion graphs appear in almost every Paper 2 - practice reading gradients and areas
Always state the direction when giving velocity as an answer
Show all steps in calculations even for simple arithmetic
Remember to convert units if necessary (km/h to m/s: divide by 3.6)
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Distance-time and velocity-time graphs showing straight lines for uniform motion and curved lines for accelerated motion, with annotations showing how to find speed/acceleration from gradients
